mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal consumption expenditures as measured by RSAFS reflect strong demand, driving corporate earnings growth and pushing equity prices higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or upward RSAFS trend fails to produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE exceeds 5.0% but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A mismatch between rising retail sales (RSAFS) and stagnant industrial production (INDPRO) signals demand outpacing supply, leading to inventory building and temporary earnings optimism that boosts S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When RSAFS growth exceeds INDPRO growth by more than 1% month-over-month, five-day S&P 500 returns are not significantly positive (mean < 0, or win rate < 55%).

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at elevated levels above 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that depresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not negative on average (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the signal generates less than 55% negative instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent increases in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) erode real purchasing power and raise uncertainty about future monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for CPIAUCSL monthly changes against 20-day S&P 500 forward returns is above -0.03 or the correlation is positive (Pearson r > 0).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1,200 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment and weakening economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1200, 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of cases or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) combined with a low unemployment rate (UNRATE below 5.0%) signals a tight labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.0 and PAYEMS increases month-over-month, the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return is not positive in more than 55% of instances or the average return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below a 3-month moving average activates a supply-side contraction signal that indicates lower economic output and earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO drops below its 3-month moving average, the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is negative in less than 55% of instances or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
